hints.text: Plates large & **white** (passenger) / **yellow** (commercial)

shared_with.note: Laos uses **yellow plates on regular vehicles** — within SE Asia, yellow *commercial* plates are Thailand-only.
shared_with.diff: Yellow on ordinary cars + right-hand traffic → Laos.

hints.text: Drives on the **left**
Bollard **square with pointed top**, black-and-white stripes
Signposts **square & bulky**, usually white with **black-painted bottoms**
hints.uniq_note: Within Southeast Asia this signpost design is unique to Thailand (per Plonk It) — outside SEA check other clues.

hints.text: **Concrete roads** with a large square pattern are common

shared_with.note: Concrete square-pattern roads are common there too.

hints.text: Poles **square with small holes** running vertically

shared_with.diff: Sinhala script + left-low-cam Gen4 → Sri Lanka.

hints.text: **Thai script**: compact, many fonts have **tiny circles** in the letters

shared_with.note: Khmer has **longer lines + more accents underneath**.
Lao script looks very similar to Thai.
shared_with.diff: Check the **driving side**: left = Thailand, right = Laos. Lao stop signs have 2 characters (Thai 3).

hints.text: **Yellow middle lines** (solid or dashed)

shared_with.diff: Yellow centre line → Thailand, not Malaysia.

hints.text: Street-light poles with **red-black or black-white striped bottoms**; distinct **long thin lamp posts** hanging over the road
Small-road directional signs: white **two-part** (arrow below, info above), road number with **Garuda**; highways green with Latin under the Thai names
**Stop signs in Thai script** (Laos: similar but only 2 characters instead of 3)
Road markers with **white Garuda = national roads** (white/black on the map), **black Garuda = province roads** (blue/yellow on the map)
Streets lined with **flags** — national + yellow **royal flags**
Distinctive **green village signs** all around the country
Most Gen3 has **no visible car** (rare: bike trekkers, white car with brake light)

shared_with.note: Vientiane Gen3 also shows no car.
Some Cambodian Gen3 shows no car.

hints.text: **Area codes**: Bangkok = 02, others 3 digits, geographically grouped — first digit already helps (mobiles 08/09, 06 non-regional)
**National road first digit** → region: 1 = north, 2 = northeast, 3 = centre, 4 = south (1–2 digit highways can cross regions)
Provincial roads: **2-letter Thai province abbreviation** on waystones & signs (never on national-road ones); black boxes carry **3-letter abbreviations** (ABC1234 or TCC-L2-ABC format)
**Far south = Muslim-majority**: Islamic attire, Jawi (Arabic-based) script, mosques

**Landscape metas**: flat & dry → north of Bangkok + east (east feels drier); tall mountains → northwest (+parts of peninsula); low smooth forested → centre-north; steep exposed rock → west + peninsula; red soil → mostly northeast; gravel roads → mapped areas

**Plantation metas (south)**: rubber (black taps on trunks) mostly south; >90% of palm oil in the south; areca palms on the peninsula (esp. Ranong); garden croton (yellow/orange/red leaves) around Ranong & coasts
**Rice**: main belt mapped; dry-season rice → central plains (dry season = wildfire haze in the sky); wet-season rice → eastern plains
**Coconut/palm metas**: dense short-coconut plantations → west of Bangkok; tall-coconut plantations → Prachuap Khiri Khan; masses of palmyra palms → east peninsula coast (S of Bangkok + far south)

**Crop metas**: sugarcane → central/east; cassava (digitate leaves) → central + NE plains; corn → north/central; fruit → Chiang Mai–Lamphun valley + E-Sa-Kaeo/Chanthaburi; durian → Chanthaburi; pineapple → SE & SW of Bangkok; eucalyptus plantations → Sa Kaeo; teak (big leaves, yellow flowers) → north; Tenasserim pines → north/northwest only
**Grey corrugated metal roofs** → mostly north & south

**Pole metas**: Chilean-style indented poles → around Bangkok (thicker versions on highways everywhere); insulators with **7+ ridges** → southern peninsula + Chiang Rai/Phayao highways (5–6 = standard); **1 insulator** on crossbar → peninsula south of Ranong/Chumphon, **2** → rest of country
**Wind turbines** → W-Chaiyaphum & W-Nakhon-Ratchasima + coast of Songkhla/Nakhon Si Thammarat
Black bollard with **three large white reflectors**
**Phuket**: touristy & wealthy — modern buildings, resorts, heavy traffic among hills; **Koh Samui & Koh Pha-Ngan**: narrow crowded tropical roads
Taxis **pink** and **yellow-on-green**; street signs with small green section under blue (Bangkok & Nonthaburi)
hints.uniq_note: Yellow-on-green taxis may rarely appear elsewhere — but **pink taxis are practically exclusive** to Bangkok.

hints.text: Red **songthaew** trucks (shared-taxi pickups)
Street signs **shaped like a ship's helm**
**Red street signs with golden decorated borders** in Mueang Chiang Rai (blue variants exist)

**Northern spotlights**: Phrao valley (flat, ringed by mountains, rice+fruit); Doi Luang Chiang Dao (3rd-tallest, distinct shape); Phayao's long jagged N-S ridge; W-Tak rolling corn/sugarcane hills w/ very brown pavement; Phetchabun valley flanked by N-S ranges

**Central/NE spotlights**: Kanchanaburi deep-red cassava/rubber fields; SW-Lopburi distinct mountains; N-Saraburi E-W exposed-rock ranges; Chaiyaphum long flat ridges; Khao Yai NP tourist strip + rocky hills (camera **smudge** on the forest road!); N-Nakhon-Phanom karst views (on the Lao side); Mukdahan long low hills in mixed fields; SE-Nakhon-Ratchasima & S-Sisaket brown paved roads in crop fields
**Southern spotlights**: Khao Sam Roi Yot NP rock massifs (visible from the province centre); Khao Sok NP — tallest/steepest southern rock mountains (E-W); W-Krabi/E-Phang-nga N-S rock ranges

**Grid spotlights**: S-Lopburi NNW grid (rice/corn/sugarcane/cassava, ridges N/S/W); Pathum Thani near-perfect N-S grid; W-Rayong NE-SW industrial grid (rubber/cassava/pineapple); Highway 44 (Krabi–Surat Thani): divided 4-lane w/ huge median — often only a yellow inner line betrays it
**Rare cars**: visible-mirror car → only far southern Thailand (mostly overwritten by Gen4); **Ko Sichang** island car with bars protruding from the blur
